name: gth-regression-b1-b6
note: Regression cover for the GTH.B1-B6 fixes, driven against the observatory's real geometry (the window runs 1290x810 even though project.godot declares 1280x800 -- trust the rects). HISTORY, because it explains the shape of this file: this suite used to use a genuine fish-bowl LAYOUT BUG as its B1 fixture. Once a day had run, the `hash` readout widened from a dash to 16 hex digits, and that min-width shock propagated to the root VBox and shoved `btn-storylets` off the right edge -- so the suite asserted `on_screen: false` and went green on it for a release. It had encoded a defect as expected behaviour: the assertion that should have CAUGHT the bug was instead pinning it, and fixing the layout is what turned this suite red. The B1 assertions below now pin the INVARIANT the app actually wants -- every top-bar control fully on screen, before AND after the hash populates -- which is the test that would have caught the original bug on day one. KNOWN COVERAGE GAP, stated rather than papered over: with the layout fixed, this scenario no longer exercises B1's on_screen:false / visible_fraction / clipped / clamped-anchor path at all, because the observatory no longer has a clipped control to point at. That path is B1's actual subject and it is now uncovered here. It needs a control that is off-screen ON PURPOSE, which belongs in the addon's own project-agnostic test scene -- not in this app, since the only way to restore the fixture here would be to reintroduce the defect. Fishbowl-owned fixture; the addon stays project-agnostic.

steps.note: B5: expect resize_locked=true, mode=windowed, size 1290x810, no window_warning
B1 baseline: before a day runs, the button is wholly inside the viewport

the hash is now 16 hex digits. It lives in the fixed-width monospace status strip and is clip_text'd, so its width is no longer a layout demand at all -- this read is what makes the two assertions below meaningful.
THE REGRESSION: expect on_screen=true, visible_fraction=1.0, clipped=[], and rect_px x=1134 -- the SAME x as before the dawn. A populated hash must move nothing.

The invariant, asserted where the bug used to live: the rightmost top-bar control stays wholly inside the viewport after the hash populates. This is the assertion whose absence let a debug readout resize the application.

...and the deliverable it used to starve. `summary` reported clipped:[right] visible_fraction:0.801 from first dawn onward -- every line of the app's primary output cut mid-word. The rightmost column no longer absorbs an overflow, because there is no overflow.

steps.label: b2-region-array
steps.note: B2: the declared shape

steps.label: b2-region-json-string
steps.note: B2: the shape that used to throw a GDScript type error from four frames down

steps.label: b2-region-garbage
steps.note: B2: must come back a NAMED error, not a crash

steps.label: b2-region-offframe
steps.note: B2: outside the frame -- named error, not a zero-size get_region crash
B3: result.repeat must read 2. F9 toggles the fish-bowl's own DevHarness, so twice = net no-op.
M5 / Mode B: the observed gui_input trace must name who REALLY received the click and score itself against Mode A -- expect agrees_with_mode_a=true on an unambiguous top-level button
M5 / Mode B over the roster Tree: prediction vs observation on a location click

The click must LAND and open the dialog -- which is what B4 below needs. This step used to be B1's clamped-anchor proof (the old raw-centre anchor aimed at x=1321, outside the window, and missed while reporting success); the anchor is now at x=1179, wholly inside, so no clamping happens and that path is NOT covered here. See the coverage gap in the header note.
B4: this point is over the storylets AcceptDialog. Expect viewport=embedded_window + the window's own contents -- NOT a main-viewport control that cannot receive the click.
B4: the dialog's controls should carry in_embedded_window

steps.label: b6-pre
steps.note: B6 EXPERIMENT begins -- establish a known frame
B6: the clock advanced, so the frame SHOULD differ from b6-pre. Input is app-level and unaffected by minimize.

steps.label: b6-while-minimized
steps.note: B6 ANSWERED, 2026-07-16: this sha comes back byte-identical to b6-pre even though the clock above advanced a slot -- the framebuffer freezes while minimized while the sim keeps running. With the default if_changed:true it would have deduped to `changed: false`, i.e. the harness reporting 'nothing happened' about a clock that had moved. That is why the guard below exists. Left in as the standing proof; flip allow_minimized off and the guard takes over.

steps.label: b6-after-restore
steps.note: B6: the true current frame, for comparison against b6-while-minimized

steps.label: b6-guarded
steps.note: B6: the guard must restore the window and set window_was_minimized -- never hand back a frame it cannot vouch for
B5/B6 close-out: mode should be back to windowed, resize still locked, size unchanged
